sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Manchester City are in excellent form, unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 draws, showcasing strong attacking prowess with 11 goals scored. Fulham, on the other hand, have had a mixed run with 2 wins and 3 losses in their last 5 games, conceding in every match and struggling defensively with 8 goals against.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Manchester City have dominated Fulham, securing victories in both matches (5-4 and 2-0). City have scored 7 goals against Fulham in these games, while conceding 4 in the most recent high-scoring clash, indicating potential for goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Erling Haaland remains a standout for Manchester City, with 2 goals and 2 assists in his last 5 matches, and a historical average rating of 7.88 against Fulham. For Fulham, Harry Wilson is in form, averaging a 7.52 rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 1 assist, posing a threat on the counter.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester City top the league in goals scored (51) and boast the second-best defense with only 24 goals conceded, alongside a high possession average of 59.2%. Fulham sit mid-table (12th) with 35 goals scored and 37 conceded, often struggling against top teams due to defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Manchester City's superior form, historical dominance over Fulham, and Sofascore's prediction of a 78% chance of a home win, they are clear favorites at the Etihad Stadium. Fulham's inability to keep clean sheets (8 games without one) suggests City’s attack will likely capitalize, though Fulham may find the net given City's recent defensive lapses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half dominance by Manchester City
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City have asserted their dominance in the first half at the Etihad Stadium, leading Fulham 3-0 at halftime. The home side showcased their attacking prowess with clinical finishing, capitalizing on their opportunities with goals from Antoine Semenyo, Nico O'Reilly, and Erling Haaland. Their control of possession and ability to penetrate the final third repeatedly overwhelmed Fulham's defense. City have been relentless in creating big chances and converting them effectively, leaving little room for Fulham to respond.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Fulham's struggle to find rhythm
halftimeSections.text: Fulham, on the other hand, have struggled to find their footing in this matchup. Despite a few fleeting moments in the attacking third, they have been largely outplayed, failing to win possession in dangerous areas. Their defense has been under constant pressure, with limited success in aerial and ground duels. A missed big chance highlights their difficulty in making the most of rare opportunities, and they will need a significant shift in strategy to mount a comeback in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Manchester City maintain consistent attacking pressure for the majority of the time, with several sustained spells of dominance in Fulham's half. Early on, there were brief moments where Fulham tried to push forward, but these were quickly snuffed out as City took control. As the half progressed, City's attacking waves became more frequent and intense, particularly around the times of their goals. Fulham showed occasional bursts of intent, especially towards the end of the half, but lacked the consistency to truly threaten City's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers in the first half
halftimeSections.text: Antoine Semenyo has been a standout for Manchester City, contributing both a goal and an assist, while Erling Haaland remains a constant threat up front with his goal-scoring instinct. Nico O'Reilly also made his mark with a well-taken goal, adding to City's commanding lead. For Fulham, players like Harry Wilson and Raúl Jiménez showed glimpses of potential in attack, but their efforts were not enough to break through City's solid backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Manchester City are likely to continue their aggressive approach, seeking to extend their lead further. Given their dominance in possession and final third entries, they may look to exploit any fatigue or frustration in Fulham's ranks. Fulham, however, will need to regroup defensively and take risks in attack if they are to salvage anything from this game. A more direct approach or tactical substitutions could help them find a way back, though it remains a steep challenge against a team of City's caliber.
